output: {"match_id":"m014","winner":"A","rationale":"A is the sharper redundant-tool-invocation seed: it creates a realistic conflict between a blanket live-verification policy and cached context, where rechecking would be actively misleading because the old pod is gone and the new pod has no termination history. B is solid but more didactic, with TTL/rate-limit making the no-recall choice comparatively explicit."}
